title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
external_feedback:
Hey - I've found 3 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- You’ve introduced DefaultTrustFlushSchedule as the single source of truth, but a lot of tests and docs still hardcode "0 * * * *"; consider reusing the constant (where possible in Go code) to reduce the risk of drift if the default is ever changed.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- You’ve introduced DefaultTrustFlushSchedule as the single source of truth, but a lot of tests and docs still hardcode "0 * * * *"; consider reusing the constant (where possible in Go code) to reduce the risk of drift if the default is ever changed.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go" line_range="1702-1711" />
<code_context>
+func TestIntegration_TrustFlush_SuspendTruePreservesCronJob(t *testing.T) {
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**suggestion (testing):** Consider also asserting the CronJob schedule remains unchanged when suspending

To better enforce the "pause without changing cadence" behavior, also assert that the CronJob’s `spec.schedule` remains equal to the original value (e.g. `0 * * * *`). This will help catch regressions where the reconciliation logic might accidentally change the schedule when toggling suspend.

Suggested implementation:

```golang
	createPrerequisites(t, ctx, c, ns.Name)

	// Assert that the trust flush CronJob schedule is preserved when suspending
	cronJob := &batchv1.CronJob{}
	g.Expect(c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{
		Namespace: ns.Name,
		// NOTE: adjust this name if the CronJob resource is created with a different name
		Name: "trust-flush",
	}, cronJob)).To(Succeed())
	g.Expect(cronJob.Spec.Schedule).To(Equal("0 * * * *"))

```

1. Ensure the following imports are present at the top of `integration_test.go`:
   - `batchv1 "k8s.io/api/batch/v1"`
   - `sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client`
2. If the trust flush CronJob is created with a different name (e.g. including a prefix/suffix), update `"trust-flush"` in the `ObjectKey` to match the actual name or use the same naming helper used in the controller/tests.
3. If this test already retrieves the CronJob later in the function, you may instead reuse that `CronJob` object and only add the `g.Expect(cronJob.Spec.Schedule).To(Equal("0 * * * *"))` assertion next to the existing suspend-related assertions to avoid duplicate `Get` calls.
</issue_to_address>

### Comment 2
<location path="operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_trustflush_test.go" line_range="618-627" />
<code_context>
+func TestReconcileTrustFlush_WebhookDefaulted_CreatesCronJobAndReportsReady(t *testing.T) {
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**suggestion (testing):** Assert CronJob spec reflects the webhook-defaulted TrustFlushSpec (schedule/suspend)

To better guard against regressions, also assert that `cronJob.Spec.Schedule` equals `ks.Spec.TrustFlush.Schedule` and that `cronJob.Spec.Suspend` is nil or `false`, so the CronJob configuration is explicitly tied to the defaulted spec rather than only its existence.
</issue_to_address>

### Comment 3
<location path="docs/reference/keystone-crd.md" line_range="144" />
<code_context>
+| `trustFlush` | [`*TrustFlushSpec`](#trustflushspec) | No | `{schedule: "0 * * * *", suspend: false}` (materialized by the defaulting webhook) | Trust flush CronJob configuration. Default-on: when the field is omitted, the defaulting webhook populates a hourly schedule so `keystone-manage trust_flush` runs by default; there is no nil-back path on a webhook-enabled cluster (a `kubectl patch ... 'spec/trustFlush'='null'` round-trips through admission and is re-materialized). To pause without deleting the CronJob, set `suspend: true` — the resource and `TrustFlushReady=True` condition are preserved (CC-0057, CC-0096). |
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**suggestion (typo):** Use "an hourly" instead of "a hourly" for correct grammar.

Since "hourly" begins with a vowel sound, the article should be "an". Please update the phrase to "populates an hourly schedule".

```suggestion
| `trustFlush` | [`*TrustFlushSpec`](#trustflushspec) | No | `{schedule: "0 * * * *", suspend: false}` (materialized by the defaulting webhook) | Trust flush CronJob configuration. Default-on: when the field is omitted, the defaulting webhook populates an hourly schedule so `keystone-manage trust_flush` runs by default; there is no nil-back path on a webhook-enabled cluster (a `kubectl patch ... 'spec/trustFlush'='null'` round-trips through admission and is re-materialized). To pause without deleting the CronJob, set `suspend: true` — the resource and `TrustFlushReady=True` condition are preserved (CC-0057, CC-0096). |
```
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

***
